scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every repository-scoped endpoint (/repos/{owner}/{repo}/...) in GitHub REST API v3 1.1.4 is enumerated below (500 real operations, plus 2 close_issue/close_pull_request rows documenting write-action reuse of the update_issue/update_pull_request endpoint, plus the github_app AuthHook token-exchange endpoint). Org-level, user-level, enterprise-level, and Enterprise Cloud/Server admin surfaces (orgs/*, teams/*, enterprise-*, user/*, gists/*, GraphQL) remain out of scope: this connector is configured with a single owner+repo (spec.json), not an org or user identity, so org/user-scoped resources have no natural config surface to hang off of without a scope-widening spec.json change (a distinct follow-on, not this pass).

operation.reason: auth token-exchange endpoint used internally by the github_app AuthHook, not a syncable data stream or a directly caller-invoked write action
repository deletion is never a reverse-ETL action

full repository settings PATCH (visibility, default branch, has_issues/wiki/pages toggles, etc.) is repo-admin configuration, not reverse-ETL record data; narrower settings writes (topics, environments, webhooks, rulesets, branch protection) are each covered individually where a real write action exists
